findings.title: Duplicate ticket detected: SEARCH-202
findings.description: SEARCH-202 is a duplicate of SEARCH-201. Reason: Both tickets report issues related to search relevance and user satisfaction after the v2.4.1 release.

findings.title: Risk hotspot detected: auth
findings.description: Detected auth risk keywords in 4 source(s): DOC-002, DOC-003, SEARCH-203, SEARCH-206

findings.title: Risk hotspot detected: pricing
findings.description: Detected pricing risk keywords in 3 source(s): DOC-001, DOC-002, SEARCH-201

findings.title: Risk hotspot detected: accessibility
findings.description: Detected accessibility risk keywords in 1 source(s): DOC-002

summary: The SearchBoost Query Engine is experiencing a significant 15% drop in search relevance scores following the recent deployment of version 2.4.1, which has raised concerns among users about the accuracy and efficiency of search results. Key user pain points include increased search latency on mobile web, stale autocomplete suggestions, and missing analytics for new query types, indicating potential issues with the new ranking algorithm and overall system performance. The most important signals from the data highlight risk hotspots in authentication, pricing, and accessibility, suggesting areas that require immediate attention to restore user trust and satisfaction.
